repo.name: iterativv/NostalgiaForInfinity
repo.description: Trading strategy for the Freqtrade crypto bot

recommendations.label: Timeframe
recommendations.note: README warns not to override the strategy timeframe.

recommendations.label: Open trades
recommendations.note: The project recommends this range with unlimited stake sizing.

recommendations.label: Pairlist size
recommendations.note: Volume-based pairlists are called out as a good fit.

recommendations.label: Markets
recommendations.note: Stable coin pairs are preferred over BTC or ETH quote pairs.

recommendations.label: Leveraged tokens
recommendations.note: BULL, BEAR, UP, DOWN style leveraged tokens should be excluded.

entry.label: Trend filter
entry.note: Avoids entries when the broader trend context is weak.

entry.label: Pullback setup
entry.note: Looks for discounted entries inside a larger trend.

entry.label: Volume confirmation
entry.note: Reduces false positives from thinly traded pairs.

exit.label: Profit protection
exit.note: Locks in gains when momentum weakens.

exit.label: Emergency loss cut
exit.note: Closes failed trades before losses expand.

protection.label: Cooldown
protection.note: Prevents immediate re-entry after a trade.

protection.label: Signal protection
protection.note: Recent release notes repeatedly add protection to specific X6/X7 signals.

releaseTimeline.details: Latest GitHub release adds protection to many X6 and X7 signals and refreshes blacklist configs.
Current branch emphasizes small strategy adjustments and frequent config maintenance.
The repository exposes X6 and X7 as the most visible current-generation strategy files.

backtestChecklist.reason: Official docs point to a separate historical data repository for faster local backtests.
Tests need an exchange context before running.
Docs distinguish spot and futures market runs.
A bounded timerange keeps comparisons reproducible.
The documented analysis script runs the target backtest suite.

configGuards.name: exit_profit_only
configGuards.impact: Allows exits that protect capital instead of only profitable exits.

configGuards.name: ignore_roi_if_entry_signal
configGuards.impact: Matches the strategy interaction between ROI and entries.

riskMatrix.mitigation: Use out-of-sample periods and avoid tuning only for a single market month.
Use volume pairlists and avoid thin markets.
Blacklist BULL, BEAR, UP, DOWN, and similar instruments.
Do not override required strategy variables, especially timeframe and exit flags.
